questions.question: 11 (a) A student has a model electric railway. The model railway uses a step-down transformer. The input voltage is 230 V. The transformer has 1710 turns on the input coil and 90 turns on the output coil. Calculate the output voltage of the transformer. output voltage = .......................................................V [3] (b) A step-up tr
questions.text: 11 (a) A student has a model electric railway. The model railway uses a step-down transformer. The input voltage is 230 V. The transformer has 1710 turns on the input coil and 90 turns on the output coil. Calculate the output voltage of the transformer. output voltage = .......................................................V [3] (b) A step-up tr

parts.answer: In a step-down transformer there are fewer turns on secondary / output coil (than on primary / input coil) In a step-up transformer there are more turns on secondary / output coil (than on primary / input coil) B2 / power wasted (in cables ) / more efficient (transmission) B1 And any one from: (because) smaller current (in transmission cables) (and so) smaller heating effect (in transmission cables) (and so) thinner cables can be used (which are cheaper) B1 0625/31 October/November 2018 B1 2. 6 B1 3. 8 B1
Any three from: (nucleus has) same number protons or same atomic / proton number same charge different mass different nucleon number different number of neutrons B3
